summary: REQ-12 appears implemented: Company Snapshot currency labels shown in the diff were changed to (₹), and currency-formatting helpers were updated to output INR/₹. REQ-13 and REQ-15 have evidence for percent-type storage/validation and query parsing, but the required exact UI labels and percent-sign display are not visible in the provided truncated form diff. REQ-14’s label change to “6 Months” is not evidenced in the shown diff content.

missing_requirements.requirement: Treat "Promoter Pledge Quantity" as a percentage field and display a percent sign for it in the Company Snapshot form (label must be "Promoter Pledge Quantity (%)").
missing_requirements.reason: While the data model, validation, and query parsing indicate this field is treated as a percent (0–1) internally, the diff content provided does not show the actual form label text or that the UI displays a percent sign for this field (the relevant section of the form is truncated).

missing_requirements.requirement: Update the user-facing label "Result Updated Since 6 Years" to "Result Updated Since 6 Months" everywhere it appears in the UI, without changing the existing state key/shareable link behavior.
missing_requirements.reason: The diff does not show any occurrence of the label text being updated to "6 Months"; the form section where this label would appear is not visible in the provided truncated snippet.

missing_requirements.requirement: Treat "Net NPA (NBFC)" as a percentage field and display a percent sign for it in the Company Snapshot form (label must be "Net NPA (NBFC) (%)").
missing_requirements.reason: The diff shows percent-range validation and percent-aware query parsing for netNpaNbfc, but the exact UI label "Net NPA (NBFC) (%)" and percent-sign display are not visible in the provided truncated form diff.

hallucinated_features.description: Backward-compatible parsing for percentage query parameters (treat values > 1 as old whole-percent format and convert to decimal) for pledge and npa.
hallucinated_features.reason: The request required preserving existing query parameter keys and not breaking existing shared links, but it did not explicitly request adding heuristic backward-compatibility conversion logic based on numeric magnitude.
